environment_options.name: Cold garage (5–15°C)
environment_options.desc: Cool room below 18°C or near windows — may affect adhesion.
warnings: Cold environment compensation applied — nozzle +5°C, first layer bed +7°C, first layer speed reduced 20%.
Preheat the enclosure for 15 minutes before starting. Keep door closed throughout print.

environment_options.name: Very cold (<5°C)
environment_options.desc: Below 5°C — garage or outdoor printing, significant warping risk.
warnings: Very cold environment — nozzle +10°C, bed +5°C, first layer speed reduced 30%.
Extended preheat (20 min) strongly recommended. Monitor first layer adhesion closely.
Cold environments often have high humidity — check and dry filament before printing.

environment_options.name: High humidity
environment_options.desc: Above 60% humidity — moisture-sensitive materials need drying.
warnings: High humidity — dry all filaments before printing. Moisture causes bubbling, stringing, and weak layer bonding.
Store opened filament in sealed bags or dry boxes with desiccant between prints.
